subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Jupiter's Great Red Spot
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Jupiter's Great Red Spot.

hook.text: A storm larger than Earth has been raging on Jupiter for at least 350 years. It's the Great Red Spot.

buildup.text: Winds inside reach 680 km/h. The spot is an anticyclonic storm powered by Jupiter's internal heat.

discovery.text: First recorded in 1665, it has been continuously observed since the 1830s.

twist.text: The Great Red Spot is shrinking. In the 1800s it was three Earths wide. Now it's barely one.

climax.text: Why it has lasted centuries and why it's red remain open mysteries in planetary science.

punchline.text: A hurricane bigger than Earth. Centuries old. Still spinning.

quiz.question: What is happening to Jupiter's Great Red Spot over time?
options: It is slowly shrinking in size
It is growing larger and more intense
It is splitting into two separate storms
